meta.title: MCP | kalandra.tech — Talk to This Site from Your AI Assistant
meta.description: Connect Claude or any MCP-compatible AI assistant to kalandra.tech: submit job offers, browse blog posts, and read or write comments over the Model Context Protocol.

hero.description: This site runs its own Model Context Protocol server. Connect it to Claude or any MCP-compatible assistant, and the assistant can act here on your behalf — submit a job offer, follow the conversation about it, browse the blog, and join the comments. Every action goes through the same public API the website itself uses.

connect.title: Connect
connect.transportNote: Streamable HTTP, stateless. Reading the blog needs no account; tools that act as you expect your access token in the Authorization header.
connect.tokenSignedOut: Sign in and your access token gets filled into the command above — that's what lets the assistant act as your account.
connect.tokenSignedIn: You're signed in. The copy button above includes your current access token.
connect.tokenNote: Access tokens are short-lived (about an hour) — when the assistant starts getting 401s, grab a fresh one here.

tools.name: submit_job_offer
tools.description: Send me a job offer — the same flow as the Hire Me form.

tools.name: list_my_job_offers
tools.description: Your submitted offers with their current status.

tools.name: get_job_offer_comments
tools.description: The conversation thread on one of your offers.

tools.name: add_job_offer_comment
tools.description: Reply in that thread — I get notified by email.

tools.name: list_blog_posts
tools.description: Published posts with summaries and tags, straight from the RSS feed.

tools.name: get_blog_post_comments
tools.description: The public comment thread of any post.

tools.name: post_blog_comment
tools.description: Comment on a post, or reply to someone, as you.

tools.name: get_my_comments
tools.description: Everything you've said here — blog and job-offer comments — with the replies they received.

examples.title: Try asking
prompts: Submit a job offer to Pavel: senior .NET engineer at Acme, remote, salary 120–150k.
What has Pavel written on his blog lately?
Did anyone reply to my comments on kalandra.tech?

how.title: How it works
items: The tools are a second front door on the API: each call runs the same code the website's own requests do, as your account, with the same validation, rate limits, and email notifications.
Blog posts are read from the site's RSS feed, so the assistant sees exactly what is published.
Built in .NET with the official MCP C# SDK, served as a /mcp route on the API itself — no separate service.
